# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent kong no auth.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: kong_no_auth

_format_version: "3.0"
services:
  - name: user-service
    url: http://user-service:8000
    routes:
      - name: user-route
        paths:
          - /users
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
        protocols:
          - https
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_names:
            - apikey
          hide_credentials: true

  - name: order-service
    url: http://order-service:8000
    routes:
      - name: order-route
        paths:
          - /orders
        methods:
          - GET
          - POST
        protocols:
          - https
    plugins:
      - name: key-auth
        config:
          key_names:
            - apikey
          hide_credentials: true

upstreams:
  - name: user-upstream
    targets:
      - target: user-service:8000
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /health
        healthy:
          interval: 10
          successes: 2
        unhealthy:
          interval: 10
          http_failures: 3

  - name: order-upstream
    targets:
      - target: order-service:8000
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /health
        healthy:
          interval: 10
          successes: 2
        unhealthy:
          interval: 10
          http_failures: 3

plugins:
  - name: rate-limiting
    config:
      second: 5
      policy: local

consumers:
  - username: api-consumer
    keyauth_credentials:
      - key: YOUR_SECURE_API_KEY_HERE
